---

title: "D02_02: Filtered Sales Views"
subtitle: "Create virtual views for each filter condition combination"
parent: "D02_filtered_customer_views"
chapter: "CH12"
category: "derivation-task"
number: "D02_02"
date-created: "2025-12-24"
date-modified: "2025-12-24"
author: "Claude"
type: "derivation-task"
depends_on:
  - "D02_01_condition_grid"
outputs_to:
  - "cleansed_data.filtered.df_sales_{condition_key}___cleansed"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
---

# D02_02: Filtered Sales Views {#overview}

This task creates virtual views for each filter condition combination in the condition grid. Each view applies the appropriate time, product, geographic, and source filters to the base sales data.

## View Creation (NSQL) {#view-creation}

```nsql
# D02_02: Create Filtered Sales Views [Layer: 4CL]
CREATE_FILTERED_VIEWS D02_02:
  FOR EACH condition IN condition_grids:
    # Define view name based on condition key
    view_name: "df_sales_" + condition.condition_key + "___cleansed"

    # Create virtual view with all filters applied
    # Read from 3PR processed_data, output to 4CL cleansed_data
    CREATE VIEW cleansed_data.filtered.{view_name} AS:
      FROM processed_data.df_{condition.source_filter}_sales___processed

      # Apply time filter
      FILTER:
        CASE condition.time_condition
          WHEN 'now' THEN TRUE  # No time restriction
          WHEN 'm1year' THEN transaction_date >= (time_now - INTERVAL '1 year')
          WHEN 'm1quarter' THEN transaction_date >= (time_now - INTERVAL '3 months')
          WHEN 'm1month' THEN transaction_date >= (time_now - INTERVAL '1 month')
        END

      # Apply product line filter
      AND:
        CASE condition.product_line_id_filter
          WHEN 0 THEN TRUE  # All products
          ELSE product_line_id = condition.product_line_id_filter
        END

      # Apply geographic filter
      AND:
        CASE condition.state_filter
          WHEN 'ALL' THEN TRUE  # All states
          ELSE state = condition.state_filter
        END

      # Add filter columns for reference
      SELECT *, condition.time_condition, condition.product_line_id_filter,
             condition.state_filter, condition.source_filter

    # Layer 4CL: Output to cleansed_data with ___cleansed suffix
    LOG "Created view: {view_name}"
```

**Layer**: 4CL (Cleanse) - Per MP144, output uses `___cleansed` suffix.

## Filter Implementation by Type {#filter-types}

### Time Condition Filtering

```nsql
FILTER sales_data BY
  CASE
    WHEN time_condition == "now" THEN TRUE  # All data
    WHEN time_condition == "m1year" THEN
      transaction_date >= (time_now - INTERVAL '1 year')
    WHEN time_condition == "m1quarter" THEN
      transaction_date >= (time_now - INTERVAL '3 months')
    WHEN time_condition == "m1month" THEN
      transaction_date >= (time_now - INTERVAL '1 month')
    WHEN time_condition == "m2year" THEN
      transaction_date >= (time_now - INTERVAL '2 years')
    WHEN time_condition == "ytd" THEN
      transaction_date >= DATE_TRUNC('year', time_now)
  END
```

### Product Line Filtering

```nsql
FILTER sales_data BY
  CASE
    WHEN product_line_id_filter == 0 THEN TRUE  # All products
    ELSE product_line_id == product_line_id_filter
  END
```

### Geographic Filtering

```nsql
FILTER sales_data BY
  CASE
    WHEN state_filter == "ALL" THEN TRUE  # All states
    ELSE state == state_filter
  END
```

## Combined Filter Logic (NSQL) {#combined-filter}

```nsql
# D02_02: Combined Filter Logic Function
FUNCTION apply_all_filters(sales_data, condition):
  # Define reference time
  time_now: MAX(transaction_date) FROM sales_data

  # Apply all filters in sequence
  filtered_data: sales_data

  # Time filter
  filtered_data: FILTER filtered_data WHERE
    CASE condition.time_condition
      WHEN 'now' THEN TRUE
      WHEN 'm1year' THEN transaction_date >= DATE_SUB(time_now, INTERVAL 1 YEAR)
      WHEN 'm1quarter' THEN transaction_date >= DATE_SUB(time_now, INTERVAL 3 MONTH)
      WHEN 'm1month' THEN transaction_date >= DATE_SUB(time_now, INTERVAL 1 MONTH)
    END

  # Product filter
  filtered_data: FILTER filtered_data WHERE
    (condition.product_line_id_filter = 0 OR product_line_id = condition.product_line_id_filter)

  # Geographic filter
  filtered_data: FILTER filtered_data WHERE
    (condition.state_filter = 'ALL' OR state = condition.state_filter)

  RETURN filtered_data
```

## View Naming Convention {#naming}

```nsql
# D02 View Naming Convention Specification
DEFINE NAMING_CONVENTION:
  PATTERN: "df_sales_{source}_{time}_{product}_{state}"

  COMPONENTS:
    source: LOWER(source_filter)  # amazon, ebay, retail, cbz
    time: time_condition  # now, m1year, m1quarter, m1month
    product: product_line_id_filter  # 0, 1, 2, 3...
    state: LOWER(state_filter)  # all, ca, ny, tx...

  EXAMPLES:
    - "df_sales_amazon_now_0_all"     # Amazon, all time, all products, all states
    - "df_sales_ebay_m1quarter_2_ca"  # eBay, past quarter, product 2, California
    - "df_sales_cbz_m1month_1_ny"     # Cyberbiz, past month, product 1, New York
```

## Output View Schema {#output-schema}

```nsql
# Filtered sales view structure
SCHEMA df_sales_{condition_key}:
  COLUMNS:
    # All columns from source sales table
    customer_id: VARCHAR NOT NULL
    transaction_date: DATE NOT NULL
    product_id: VARCHAR NOT NULL
    product_line_id: INTEGER
    amount: NUMERIC
    quantity: INTEGER
    state: VARCHAR
    # ... other source columns

    # Filter condition columns (added)
    source_filter: VARCHAR NOT NULL
    time_condition: VARCHAR NOT NULL
    product_line_id_filter: INTEGER NOT NULL
    state_filter: VARCHAR NOT NULL

  TYPE: VIRTUAL VIEW
```

## Validation (NSQL) {#validation}

```nsql
# D02_02: Validate View Creation [Layer: 4CL]
VALIDATE D02_02:
  FOR EACH condition IN condition_grids:
    view_name: "df_sales_" + condition.condition_key + "___cleansed"

    CHECK view_exists:
      QUERY: "SELECT * FROM information_schema.views WHERE table_name = '{view_name}'"
      ASSERT: COUNT(*) = 1
      MESSAGE: "View {view_name} must exist"

    CHECK view_has_data:
      QUERY: "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cleansed_data.filtered.{view_name}"
      LOG: "View {view_name} has {COUNT(*)} rows"

  ON_SUCCESS: LOG "D02_02 completed: all sales views created in cleansed_data"
```

## Related Documentation

- [D02_01_condition_grid.qmd](D02_01_condition_grid.qmd) - Grid generation
- [D02_03_customer_views.qmd](D02_03_customer_views.qmd) - Customer aggregation
